syntax_2803.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2803.title: Execute Statement
syntax_2803.description:
Executes a statement on a database and optionally stores the result in a variable. Expressions embedded in the query will be escaped to avoid SQL injection.

If a single variable, such as `{test}`, is passed, the variable will be set to the number of affected rows.

If a list variable, such as `{test::*}`, is passed, the query result will be mapped to the list variable in the form `{test::<column name>::<row number>}`

Specifying `synchronously` will make skript-db execute the query on the event thread, which is useful for async events. Note that skript-db will ignore this flag if you attempt to run this on the main thread.

en:
Executes a statement on a database and optionally stores the result in a variable. Expressions embedded in the query will be escaped to avoid SQL injection.

If a single variable, such as `{test}`, is passed, the variable will be set to the number of affected rows.

If a list variable, such as `{test::*}`, is passed, the query result will be mapped to the list variable in the form `{test::<column name>::<row number>}`

Specifying `synchronously` will make skript-db execute the query on the event thread, which is useful for async events. Note that skript-db will ignore this flag if you attempt to run this on the main thread.

syntax_2804.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2804.title: Last Data Source Error
syntax_2804.description: Stores the error from the last [executed statement](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=2803), if there was one.

en: Stores the error from the last [executed statement](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=2803), if there was one.

syntax_2805.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2805.title: Data Source
syntax_2805.description:
Stores the connection information for a data source. This should be saved to a variable in a script load event or manually through an effect command.

The url format for your database may vary! The example provided uses a MySQL database.

en:
Stores the connection information for a data source. This should be saved to a variable in a script load event or manually through an effect command.

The url format for your database may vary! The example provided uses a MySQL database.

syntax_2806.creator: Blueyescat
syntax_2806.title: Unsafe Expression
syntax_2806.description: Opts out of automatic SQL injection protection for a specific expression in a statement.

en: Opts out of automatic SQL injection protection for a specific expression in a statement.
